title: Shodan host intelligence enrichment (opt-in knob)
description: The shodan_mcp knob: when a message names a host/IP, the pipeline folds in Shodan's ports/services/CVEs as a labeled context block. The batch tests BOTH that it fires when asked about a host AND that it stays quiet when a host merely appears in an unrelated ask (the 2026-07-15 relevance-note fix).

points.label: Shodan: host question folds in infrastructure data
points.summary: With the Shodan knob ON, asking about a host's exposure should cite Shodan's ports/services/org in the answer (activity shows a 'Querying Shodan' step). PASS = infrastructure facts with the Shodan step; FAIL = a generic answer that ignored the enrichment.

actions.text: Turn Shodan host intelligence ON first (this point tests that gate), then send.
What services and open ports does scanme.nmap.org expose?

points.label: Shodan: stays out of an unrelated answer (relevance-note fix)
points.summary: With Shodan ON, a compute question that merely CONTAINS a hostname must be answered with ONLY the requested result and no infrastructure aside (round-1 point #3's fix). PASS = just the hash; FAIL = any Shodan/ports/Cloudflare commentary bolted on.

actions.text: Keep Shodan ON — the point is that it should NOT intrude here.
Use the sandbox to compute the SHA-256 of the exact text hello world
